meta.name: Numbrix Series Architect Data
meta.description: Verified data for planning Numbrix puzzle book series on Amazon KDP. All printing-cost and royalty figures use Amazon KDP's official published formulas. Series structures are modeled on the volume/difficulty/grid/format patterns that dominate the live Numbrix category on Amazon (not invented themes).
sources: Amazon KDP official Printing Cost & Royalty formula (help topic G201834340)
Amazon KDP Trim Sizes specification (help topic GVBQ3CMEQW3W2VL6)
Amazon KDP June 2025 tiered royalty: 60% at list price >= $9.99, 50% below
meta.disclaimer: Printing costs and royalties are estimates based on Amazon KDP's US-marketplace black & white paperback formula. Actual page count depends on your interior layout. Always confirm the final figure in KDP's own Pricing section before publishing. This tool does not pull live sales data, BSR, or competitor ASINs.

lowPageCount.note: Books from 24 to 108 pages use a flat minimum printing cost.

standard.note: Printing cost = 1.00 + (page count x 0.012). 828 pages is the KDP B&W paperback maximum.

kdpConstants.royaltyFormula: royalty = (list price x rate) - printing cost. Expanded distribution uses 40% instead of 60%.
kdpConstants.minListPriceFormula: minimum list price = printing cost / royalty rate (so royalty never goes negative)

trimSizes.label: 6 x 9 in
trimSizes.bestFor: The default puzzle-book trim. Balanced page count and printing cost. Most Numbrix titles use this or 8.5x11.

trimSizes.label: 7 x 10 in
trimSizes.bestFor: Roomier than 6x9 — common for adult puzzle books that want bigger grids without going full letter size. Several top Numbrix sellers use 7x10.

trimSizes.label: 8.5 x 11 in (Letter)
trimSizes.bestFor: Largest standard trim. Fits multiple puzzles per page and reads as a workbook/activity book. Strong for high-volume editions and large grids.

trimSizes.label: 8.5 x 11 in (Large Print)
trimSizes.bestFor: Large Print edition for seniors and low-vision solvers. One puzzle per page, oversized numbers. Separate listing, premium price.

gridSizes.label: 6x6
gridSizes.difficultyFeel: Quickest grids. Good for beginners, warm-ups, and high-count books.

gridSizes.label: 7x7
gridSizes.difficultyFeel: Light step up from 6x6. Still approachable.

gridSizes.label: 8x8
gridSizes.difficultyFeel: Mid-range grid. Comfortable for most adult solvers.

gridSizes.label: 9x9
gridSizes.difficultyFeel: The most common 'standard' Numbrix grid on Amazon. Familiar size, broad appeal.

gridSizes.label: 10x10
gridSizes.difficultyFeel: Largest common grid. Reads as 'serious' / full-size brain training.

difficultyLevels.label: Easy
difficultyLevels.description: Lots of given numbers, short solving paths. Onboarding level.

difficultyLevels.label: Medium
difficultyLevels.description: Fewer givens, more deduction. The everyday level.

difficultyLevels.label: Hard
difficultyLevels.description: Sparse givens, longer chains. For experienced solvers.

difficultyLevels.label: Expert / Extreme
difficultyLevels.description: Minimal givens, maximum challenge. The top of the ladder.

difficultyLevels.label: Easy to Hard (Mixed)
difficultyLevels.description: A spread across levels in one book. The single most common framing in the category.

audiences.label: Adults
audiences.descriptionAngle: Screen-free logic and focus for grown-up solvers.

audiences.label: Seniors
audiences.descriptionAngle: Keep your mind active and sharp. Pairs naturally with a Large Print edition.

audiences.label: Beginners
audiences.descriptionAngle: An easy on-ramp into Numbrix with extra given numbers and clear instructions.

audiences.label: Teens, Adults & Seniors
audiences.descriptionAngle: A book the whole household can share, from teens to grandparents.

seriesBlueprints.name: Difficulty Ladder
seriesBlueprints.tagline: Same grid, climbing difficulty — the classic way readers buy the next volume.
seriesBlueprints.bestFor: Building a loyal series where solvers graduate from Easy to Expert and keep coming back.
expansionIdeas: Add a 'Vol. 2' for each level once the first ladder sells.
Release a single 'Easy to Expert' mega-book that bundles all four levels.
Spin off a Large Print version of the Easy and Medium books for seniors.

seriesBlueprints.name: Numbered Volume Series
seriesBlueprints.tagline: Same level and grid, fresh puzzles each volume (Vol. 1, Vol. 2, Vol. 3...).
seriesBlueprints.bestFor: Riding a proven format. Once one volume sells, you publish the next with the same cover system and a new number.
expansionIdeas: Keep publishing — numbered series can run to dozens of volumes.
Bundle every 3 volumes into a 'Big Book' edition at a higher price.
Swap the grid size to launch a parallel series (e.g. a 10x10 line next to your 9x9 line).

seriesBlueprints.name: Grid-Size Collection
seriesBlueprints.tagline: One difficulty, one volume per grid size (6x6, 7x7, 8x8, 9x9, 10x10).
seriesBlueprints.bestFor: Covering the whole grid spectrum so a solver can pick their preferred size — and buy more than one.
expansionIdeas: Add Easy and Hard variants of each grid edition.
Release a Large Print version of the 9x9 and 10x10 editions for seniors.
Bundle all five sizes into a single 'Complete Collection'.

seriesBlueprints.name: Regular + Large Print Pair
seriesBlueprints.tagline: Publish the same content twice: a regular edition and a Large Print edition for seniors.
seriesBlueprints.bestFor: Doubling revenue from one puzzle set. The Large Print edition is a separate listing at a premium price.
expansionIdeas: Repeat the pair for each difficulty level.
Market the Large Print edition specifically as a gift for seniors and grandparents.
Use a near-identical cover with a clear 'LARGE PRINT' banner so the pair reads as a set.

seriesBlueprints.name: Starter Empire (12-Book Plan)
seriesBlueprints.tagline: A full first-year publishing plan: four difficulties across two grids, plus large-print and a mega edition.
seriesBlueprints.bestFor: Treating Numbrix like a catalog from day one — a realistic 12-book roadmap modeled on how the category's biggest publishers operate.
expansionIdeas: Once the 9x9 and 10x10 lines sell, repeat the whole plan for 6x6 and 8x8.
Bundle each grid's four difficulties into a 'Big Book' edition.
Build a branded series name and a consistent cover template so all 12 read as one catalog.

titleFormulas.note: Templates only. Placeholders are filled from your inputs. No real ASINs or copyrighted titles are reproduced.
mainTitle: The Big Book of Numbrix: {count} {difficulty} {grid} Puzzles {audience}
Numbrix Puzzle Book {audience}: {difficultyRange} {grid} Logic Puzzles with Solutions

benefit: Train your brain with screen-free number-path logic
Sharpen focus and keep your mind active
Relax, focus, and exercise your logic
audienceTag: Perfect for adults, teens and seniors
A great gift for puzzle lovers

descriptionTemplate.note: A reusable Amazon description skeleton. Fill the blanks; rewrite in your own voice before publishing.
descriptionTemplate.hook: Love number puzzles like Sudoku and Kakuro? Then you will be hooked on Numbrix — the addictive number-path logic puzzle.
descriptionTemplate.howToPlay: The rules are simple: fill in the missing numbers from 1 to {maxNumber} so that consecutive numbers connect horizontally or vertically. No diagonals allowed.
whatsInside: Full solutions for every puzzle at the back of the book
Clear, large-enough numbers and clean printing
{trim} pages — comfortable to hold and solve
descriptionTemplate.closing: Grab your copy today and start training your brain, one number path at a time.

boxSetGuidance.note: Box sets / 'Big Book' bundles combine puzzles from several books into one larger volume at a higher price. Use this when a single volume exceeds your target page count or when you want a premium SKU.
boxSetGuidance.pageCountWarning: KDP's black & white paperback maximum is 828 pages. If a combined edition would exceed that, split it or reduce puzzles per page differently.
boxSetGuidance.pricingRule: Price a bundle below the sum of its parts but above any single volume, so the bundle reads as a deal while earning more per sale.
